prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 keyword query string (no explanation).

Reversibility constraints:
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, aliases, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not found in the inputs.
- If a candidate is uncertain or multiple candidates exist, keep them as an explicit candidate set (space-separated tokens), do not collapse or confirm them.

Optimization-usefulness constraints (missing-support recovery focus):
- Build the query by combining the essential anchors from the inputs (entity/title names) plus a short relation or attribute cue that restores the missing link (one or two tokens). Keep the query compact and BM25-friendly.

Concise behavioral patterns to apply (conservative, generalizable edits):
- Release→artist: use release title (include year if present) + release-type token (album, box set, compilation) + a relation token (artist, by, for) + candidate artist name(s).
- Organization/parent org: include parent organization name(s) + the person name + a role relation token (advisory, advisor, advisory council) and optionally the sub-entity/campaign title if present.
- Person attribute (dates): include the candidate person or owner names + role/title token (e.g., senior advisor) + a short attribute cue such as born or birth to bias toward birthdate pages.
- Actor/filmography: include actor name + candidate title(s) if present + a relation cue (cast, credits, filmography, role) and include the year token when the question provides one.
- Magazine/type queries: prefer the disambiguated entity title if present (e.g., TV Guide (magazine), Baltimore (magazine)) plus the relation token magazine or periodical.
- Sport/fight recall: include both subject and opponent names + a short relation token (defeated, beat, lost to) and any concise event token present (e.g., WBA) and/or nationality+role token (Jamaican boxer) to favor biography/athlete pages.

Representation guide:
- Think of the query as a reversible edit from a weak query: keep anchors, restore the missing relation word(s), drop extraneous verbosity or noisy side entities, and preserve uncertainty.

Style and forbidden items:
- Keep queries very short (a few keywords). Do not use Boolean operators, site:, quotes, filetype:, or other search-engine syntax.
- Do not invent facts, do not add new named entities, and avoid words that tend to surface list or peripheral pages (e.g., article, list of).

Output rules:
- Output only the single compact BM25 query string (one line). No explanations, bullets, JSON, or additional text.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Conservatively consolidated the per-sample edits into one shared prompt that preserves anchors and adds small, generalizable relation/attribute cues (release-type+artist, parent-org+advisor, person+born, actor+credits+year, disambiguated magazine titles, subject+opponent+defeated) to recover missing support. Emphasizes brevity, reversibility, preserving uncertainty, and forbids search operators so queries remain BM25-friendly.

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact BM25 second-hop search query (one-line keyword query). Output only the query.

Constraints (be conservative and reversible):
- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relation words, and uncertainty markers that appear in `question` or `summary_1`. Do not add new entities, facts, aliases, or support titles not present in the inputs.
- If a candidate is uncertain in the input, keep that uncertainty (e.g., preserve words like "?", "possible", or a candidate set) rather than asserting it.

Optimization guidance for missing-support recovery:
- Use the exact anchor terms from `summary_1` (album titles, article titles, organization names, person names) and include one concise relation token from the question that points to the missing support (examples of relation tokens: born, author, cast, filmography, performed in, defeated, for, advisory council, senior advisor, magazine, box set, album, monthly, 2013, 1994).
- When a disambiguator is present in the inputs, include it (e.g., (magazine), (album), (box set), or parenthetical years) to target the correct page.
- Favor lexical terms that identify the specific support/answer-bearing page (support page name + relation word + any relevant year or role). Avoid generic role-only queries when concrete names/titles are available.

Form and style:
- Compact keyword query, not a sentence or explanation. No boolean or site: syntax. No multiple queries. No punctuation that looks like a program.
- Keep queries short but specific: anchor + relation word(s) + minimal disambiguator/date if needed.
- Do not restate the full question or invent facts.

Output rule: produce exactly one query string and nothing else.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Kept the original conservative, reversible constraints but added targeted guidance to improve missing-support recovery: explicitly instruct to include a single concise relation token and any disambiguator or year present in inputs, and to prefer anchor+relation+minimal disambiguator format so BM25 will better find the answer-bearing page without inventing facts or using search operators.

}

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one line, no explanation).

Core constraints (preserve reversibility):
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, disambiguated titles, aliases, dates, relation words, and any uncertainty markers present in the inputs. Do not introduce new facts, entities, or aliases. If multiple candidates exist keep them as a candidate set.
- The query must be a short keyword sequence suitable for BM25. Do not use Boolean operators, site:, quotes, filetype:, or other search-engine syntax. Do not output lists, bullets, or any explanation.

Behavioral heuristics (apply conservatively to recover missing support):
- Always include the primary anchors from the inputs (entity name(s) or release/title) and add a compact relation token that matches the missing relation (example relation tokens: artist, for, by, magazine, periodical, advisory, advisor, advisory council, born, birth, cast, filmography, role, episode, defeated, beat). Prefer the disambiguated entity title when present (e.g., TV Guide (magazine)).
- If the input is a release (album/box set/compilation): combine release title [+ year if present] + release-type token (album, box set, compilation) + a relation token (artist / for / by) + candidate artist name(s).
- If the input mentions a sub‑entity or campaign/program that belongs to a parent organization: anchor the parent org name and include the person name and a tight relation token (advisory / advisor / advisory council) and optionally the campaign name if present.
- For person-attribute targets (birth date, death date, born): include the person name plus any owner/organization candidate mentioned and append an attribute cue such as born or birth.
- For actor credits: keep actor name + candidate show/film title + a compact cue (cast / filmography / role / episode) and the year token if present.
- For sports/fight queries: keep both fighter names together with a short relation token (defeated / beat / lost to) and an event or title token if present (e.g., WBA, vacant WBA).

Representation guide: think of editing a weak query into a stronger, reversible one: keep essential anchors, restore the missing relation word, retain uncertainty markers and candidate sets, and delete unrelated verbosity.

Output rules (strict):
- Output only one compact BM25 query string (single line). Use only names, titles, aliases, dates, and concise relation tokens drawn from the inputs. Do not invent facts, add operators, or output explanations.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Kept the original reversibility and compact-BM25 requirements and added a small set of conservative, generalizable heuristics mapping common missing-support patterns (release→artist, org→advisory, person→born, actor→cast/year, sports→opponent+result) to compact query templates. These behavior-level edits bias retrieval toward answer-bearing pages while preserving anchors, uncertainty, and avoiding search-operator syntax.

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one line, no explanation).

Core constraints (preserve reversibility):
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, exact titles, aliases, parenthetical disambiguators, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that appear in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, facts, aliases, or support titles not present in the inputs.
- If multiple candidate answers or works/places are present, preserve them as a candidate set rather than collapsing to one.

General optimization rule:
- Make the query expose a clear retrieval direction: keep the essential anchor(s) from the inputs and add one concise relation token that targets the missing support (e.g., release date, born/birth, population 2017, final issue, headquarters, battalion size). Keep the relation token adjacent to the anchor.

Targeted, reusable heuristics (conservative):
- Creative works (albums, books, films): prefer the canonical title form with any parenthetical disambiguator from the input (e.g., Ten (Pearl Jam album)). Add a short relation term such as "release date" or "publication date".
- Person biographies: use the canonical/full name and any parenthetical disambiguator present (e.g., Brad Anderson (wrestler)). Append a short biographical relation token such as "born", "birth", "birth date", or "year of birth".
- Organizations/corporations: start with the organization's canonical name exactly as in the input. If punctuation variants exist in the input, include a simple variant sequence (e.g., "Perkin-Elmer Perkin Elmer"). Add a concise relation/location token such as "headquarters", "headquarters Wilton Connecticut", or "company" to steer to the corporate page.
- Places/administrative areas and populations: keep the place anchor (e.g., Highgate) and include the exact containing administrative unit names listed in the summary (e.g., RM of Battle River No. 438, City of Vincent, London Borough of Haringey) plus the relation and year token (e.g., "population 2017"). If the summary mentions a broader metro name that likely holds the year statistic (e.g., Perth, Greater Perth), include that metro name as well.
- Administrative/support pages or numbered units (e.g., brigades): if the summary or missing-support hints at a specific administrative/support title (e.g., "5th Reserve Officers' Training Corps Brigade"), include that title verbatim and add concise relation terms like "largest", "battalion", "cadet enrollment", or "battalion size".

Stylistic guidance:
- Keep queries compact and lexical (anchors + 1–3 short relation/location tokens). Avoid long phrasing, generic list queries, Boolean/operators, site:, or web-search syntax.
- Favor tokens that identify the answer-bearing page (canonical title, parenthetical disambiguator, org name variant, admin unit, metro name) and the missing relation.
- Only include peripheral people/venues when the hop explicitly must verify involvement; otherwise omit them to reduce noise.

Output rules:
- Output only the one compact BM25 query string (no bullets, no explanation, no additional lines).
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Conservative, reusable additions address common missing-support failures in the minibatch: favor canonical page-title tokens and parenthetical disambiguators for works and persons, include organization canonical names (with simple variants) plus "headquarters"/location, and include exact administrative unit names and broader metro names for place/population queries. All edits preserve reversibility, keep candidate sets, and add short relation tokens so BM25 targets the answer-bearing page rather than noisy side pages.

All edits preserve reversibility, keep candidate sets, and add short relation tokens so BM25 targets the answer-bearing page rather than noisy side pages."
}

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a second-hop BM25 search query.

Requirements:
- Output exactly one compact keyword query (no explanation, no bullets, no JSON).
- The query must be a short keyword phrase suitable for BM25 retrieval, not a sentence.

Reversibility & preservation:
- Preserve all task-relevant named entities, exact titles, aliases, parenthetical disambiguators, dates, locations, and explicit uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do NOT introduce any new entities, facts, or confirmations not in the inputs.
- If multiple candidate entities are present, include them all (as a candidate set) using their exact forms or parenthetical disambiguators.

Targeting missing support:
- Always include a concise relation keyword or short phrase derived from the question to expose what to verify (e.g., release date, date of birth, population 2017, final issue, headquarters, producer credit). Prefer the exact relation wording from the question when present.
- Prefer lexical anchors that identify the answer/support page (exact article title, parenthetical disambiguator, local-government name, label, year). Include the smallest set of such anchors needed to disambiguate and find the missing support.

Style constraints:
- Be conservative and reversible: remove only irrelevant verbosity, restore any missing relation words, and keep uncertainty/candidate sets intact.
- Avoid web-search operators, Boolean program syntax, site:, or instructions to run multiple queries.
- Keep the query compact (short phrase of keywords and disambiguators). Do not invent facts.

Output rule:
- Return only the single BM25 keyword query.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: The minibatch failures show missing-support due to weak disambiguation and absent relation terms. The update instructs the model to include explicit relation words and exact disambiguating anchors (titles, parentheticals, years, locations) and to preserve candidate sets and uncertainty, which is a conservative change that should improve retrieval of the specific support page without adding facts or complex search syntax.

}

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query string (one-line keyword query; no explanation).

Core reversibility rules:
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, exact titles, aliases, parenthetical disambiguators, dates, relations, and uncertainty markers that appear in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, facts, candidate answers, or support titles not present in the input.
- If multiple candidate works/places/answers are present, keep them as a candidate set rather than collapsing to one.

Behavioral guidance (conservative, generalizable edits to improve missing-support recovery):
- Favor the answer-bearing page by anchoring the query on the primary entity title as given in inputs. Use the canonical title form and any parenthetical disambiguator present (e.g., Ten (Pearl Jam album)).
- Immediately attach a short relation token next to that anchor to recover the missing relation. Examples of concise relation tokens: release date, publication date, final, final issue, ceased publication, birth, birth date, born, population 2017, headquarters, headquarters <Town State>, cadet enrollment, battalion size, largest, single-campus.
- For creative works (albums, books, films) prefer the canonical work title + parenthetical disambiguator + relation (e.g., Ten (Pearl Jam album) release date). Avoid adding peripheral people unless the hop explicitly needs to verify involvement.
- For persons, prefer page-title style tokens and parenthetical disambiguators from `summary_1` (e.g., Brad Anderson (wrestler)), and place short biographical relation terms adjacent to the name (e.g., Brad Anderson (wrestler) born).
- For places/population queries, keep the original place anchor (e.g., Highgate) and include exact containing-area names listed in `summary_1` (verbatim administrative unit names or broader metro names when present) plus the relation and year (e.g., Highgate City of Vincent population 2017; Highgate Rural Municipality of Battle River No. 438 population 2017).
- For organizations/corporations, start the query with the organization's canonical name as given (preserve punctuation/hyphenation); you may include a simple unpunctuated variant sequence if common (e.g., Perkin‑Elmer Perkin Elmer) and append a concise relation/location term such as headquarters Wilton Connecticut or company headquarters.
- When a numbered administrative/support page (e.g., a brigade title) appears in the inputs or missing lists and is the likely support, include that brigade title verbatim and add concise relation tokens (largest, battalion, cadet enrollment) to bias retrieval to the brigade/support page.

Optimization cautions:
- Do not merely restate the whole question or produce generic role-only queries (e.g., only "producer" or "company").
- Avoid noisy list/event pages by preferring page-title tokens + short relation words rather than "list of" phrasing.
- Keep queries compact and lexical (anchors + relation tokens); avoid long explanatory language.

Output rules:
- Output only the single compact BM25 query string (one line).
- Use only concrete names, exact titles, aliases, parenthetical disambiguators, and short relation words present in the inputs.
- Do not use Boolean operators, site:, web-search syntax, or invent facts.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Integrated the small, generalizable fixes seen across the minibatch: bias queries toward canonical page-title anchors (with parenthetical disambiguators) and place a short relation token adjacent to the anchor; include exact administrative/metro names + year for population queries; prefer organization canonical names + headquarters/location tokens; and include brigade/support titles when present. These conservative edits help recover missing support pages while preserving reversibility and avoiding added noise.

- For creative works (albums, books, films) prefer the canonical work title + parenthetical disambiguator + relation (e.g., Ten (Pearl Jam album) release date). Avoid adding peripheral people unless the hop explicitly needs to verify involvement.
- For persons, prefer page-title style tokens and parenthetical disambiguators from `summary_1` (e.g., Brad Anderson (wrestler)), and place short biographical relation terms adjacent to the name (e.g., Brad Anderson (wrestler) born).
- For places/population queries, keep the original place anchor (e.g., Highgate) and include exact containing-area names listed in `summary_1` (verbatim administrative unit names or broader metro names when present) plus the relation and year (e.g., Highgate City of Vincent population 2017; Highgate Rural Municipality of Battle River No. 438 population 2017).
- For organizations/corporations, start the query with the organization's canonical name as given (preserve punctuation/hyphenation); you may include a simple unpunctuated variant sequence if common (e.g., Perkin‑Elmer Perkin Elmer) and append a concise relation/location term such as headquarters Wilton Connecticut or company headquarters.
- When a numbered administrative/support page (e.g., a brigade title) appears in the inputs or missing lists and is the likely support, include that brigade title verbatim and add concise relation tokens (largest, battalion, cadet enrollment) to bias retrieval to the brigade/support page.

Optimization cautions:
- Do not merely restate the whole question or produce generic role-only queries (e.g., only "producer" or "company").
- Avoid noisy list/event pages by preferring page-title tokens + short relation words rather than "list of" phrasing.
- Keep queries compact and lexical (anchors + relation tokens); avoid long explanatory language.

Output rules:
- Output only the single compact BM25 query string (one line).
- Use only concrete names, exact titles, aliases, parenthetical disambiguators, and short relation words present in the inputs.
- Do not use Boolean operators, site:, web-search syntax, or invent facts.",
  "rationale": "Integrated the small, generalizable fixes seen across the minibatch: bias queries toward canonical page-title anchors (with parenthetical disambiguators) and place a short relation token adjacent to the anchor; include exact administrative/metro names + year for population queries; prefer organization canonical names + headquarters/location tokens; and include brigade/support titles when present. These conservative edits help recover missing support pages while preserving reversibility and avoiding added noise."
}

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce one compact second-hop BM25 search query (a single keyword-style string). Do not output explanations.

Core constraints:
- The query must be a concise keyword query (not a sentence or explanation).
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, aliases, dates, relation words, disambiguators (e.g., parenthetical forms), and uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new facts, entities, resolved candidates, or support titles not present in the inputs.
- If multiple candidate answers are present, keep them as a candidate set by listing their tokens space-separated; do not collapse them.

Behavioral guidance to improve missing-support retrieval (conservative, generalizable edits):
- Target answer-bearing pages: when the missing support is a person, build the query around the person's canonical name plus 1–2 compact role/credit relation tokens drawn from the inputs (e.g., club, plays for, club affiliation, credits, filmography, director, storyboard, editor in chief, granted, conferred, Royal Decree). This biases retrieval toward the person/biography/credits page.
- Prefer exact disambiguated page tokens when available (e.g., use Helmet (band) rather than just Helmet) to steer to the entity page instead of noisy pages.
- For creative works (albums, films, books), include the work title verbatim plus a short type/relation cue (album, studio album, released, record label, released by) to retrieve the work’s main page rather than peripheral pages (tours, tributes, reviews).
- For publications/journals/magazines, include the university or artist plus the publication title tokens and explicit relation words like editor in chief or editor-in-chief to target editorial pages.
- Include native-language title variants when present in the input (e.g., Marquesado de Samaranch) alongside English tokens.
- Include brand/product names (e.g., Nike Hypervenom) only when needed to disambiguate; otherwise prefer person, title, or relation tokens that point to the answer-bearing page.
- De-emphasize noisy page-type tokens that commonly lead to episode lists, season pages, tour or tribute pages (e.g., season, episode, tour, review) unless those terms are the only anchors provided.

Representation advice:
- Think of editing a weak query into a stronger one: keep essential anchors (names, disambiguators, titles), restore missing relation words, remove noisy verbosity, and preserve uncertainty by listing candidate tokens.

Output rules:
- Output only a single-line BM25-style keyword query string (no quotes, no JSON, no bullets, no explanations).
- Use only tokens drawn from `question` and `summary_1` (names, titles, relation cues, dates, native-language forms); do not invent facts.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Merged the original reversible constraints with small, generalizable behavior edits observed across the minibatch: bias queries toward person/credits pages, prefer parenthetical disambiguators, include work or publication titles plus short relation cues (editor-in-chief, record label, released, plays for), and avoid noisy season/tour/tribute tokens. These conservative changes steer BM25 retrieval toward answer-bearing pages to recover missing support while preserving reversibility and compact query form.

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce exactly one compact, single-line BM25-style second-hop search query. The query must be a short keyword query (not an explanation) that best recovers the missing support page implied by summary_1.

Required behavior:
- Preserve and reuse task-relevant anchors from the input: named people, bands, albums, publications, titles, dates, and exact page titles that appear in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do NOT introduce any new entities, facts, aliases, dates, or support titles not present in the inputs.
- Keep uncertainty markers or candidate sets as given (e.g., include multiple candidate names separated by commas rather than collapsing them to one).

Focus for missing-support recovery:
- Aim the query at the specific support page you need: include one exact anchor (person/title/publication) from summary_1 and add a concise relation token or short phrase (1–4 words) that signals the needed relation or verification (examples of relation tokens: club, plays for, label, released, editor-in-chief, credits, network aired, played with, royal decree).
- If summary_1 lists a preferred page title (e.g., a publication or article name), prefer that exact title as the anchor.
- If multiple candidate anchors are present and any could retrieve the support, include the candidate set (comma separated) plus the relation token.

Style and constraints:
- Be conservative and reversible: the query should make it clear what was kept and what relation is being checked.
- Keep queries concise (few tokens) and focused on lexical anchors and a relation word—avoid restating the full question or adding explanatory words.
- Do not use web-search operators, Boolean syntax, site: or multiple separate queries.

Output rule:
- Output only the single query line.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Conservatively augments the original prompt to focus on missing-support recovery by instructing the generator to target an exact anchor from summary_1 plus a short relation token. This increases chance of retrieving the specific support page (person, publication, album, decree, etc.) while preserving reversibility and avoiding invented facts or web-search syntax.

prompt_records.updated_prompt:
Given `question` and `summary_1`, produce a single compact second-hop BM25 search query (one keyword-style string). Do not output explanations.

Reversibility constraints (must preserve):
- Preserve task-relevant named entities, titles, parenthetical disambiguators, aliases, dates, relation words, and uncertainty markers present in `question` or `summary_1`.
- Do not introduce new entities, facts, resolved candidates, or support titles not in the inputs.
- If multiple candidates or uncertainty are present, keep them as a candidate set rather than collapsing to one.

Behavioral guidance for missing-support recovery (conservative, generalizable edits):
- Target person pages when the missing support is a person: use the person's canonical name plus 1–2 concise relation/role tokens taken from the inputs (examples: club, plays for, club affiliation, biography, credits, filmography, director, storyboard, granted, conferred, Royal Decree, Boletín Oficial del Estado, editor-in-chief). This biases retrieval to the person/biography/credits page that commonly contains the needed relation.
- Prefer parenthetical disambiguators when available (e.g., Helmet (band)) to steer retrieval to the specific entity page instead of noisy album/list pages.
- For creative works (albums, films, books) include the exact work title tokens verbatim plus a short type/relation cue that signals the answer-bearing page (e.g., album, studio album, released, record label, label). Favor these over peripheral tokens (tour, tribute, review) that pull up noisy pages.
- For publications/journals combine the publication title tokens with explicit role tokens (editor-in-chief, editor in chief) and the affiliated organization/university name if present; list multiple publication tokens space-separated to preserve uncertainty.
- De-emphasize noisy page-type tokens that commonly lead to wrong pages (season, episode, discography, tour, track, review) unless those tokens are necessary and appear in the inputs.
- Include native-language title variants from the inputs when present (e.g., Marquesado de Samaranch) to improve matching.

Representation guide:
- Think of editing a weak query into a stronger one: keep essential anchors (person/work title/parenthetical title), restore the missing relation word(s), and remove noisy or peripheral terms. Keep the query compact and BM25-friendly.

Output rules (must follow):
- Output only one compact BM25 query string (no explanation, no bullets, no JSON).
- Use plain keyword tokens drawn from the inputs; do not use quoting, Boolean operators, site:, or other search-engine syntax.
- Do not invent facts or new entities.
prompt_records.prompt_update_rationale: Minibatch failures retrieved noisy brand/season/tribute pages instead of the answer-bearing person, work, or publication pages. I added small, general rules to bias queries toward person/credits pages, prefer parenthetical disambiguators, and require work/publication titles plus a short relation/type cue (editor-in-chief, record label, released, album, plays for, etc.). These conservative, behavior-level edits improve missing-support recovery while preserving reversibility and compact BM25 query format.
